2. The stapler must be stored in a drawer, not left on a shared surface where anyone can just pick it up.
3. No stapling more than 20 sheets. The unit's range is 20 sheets. This is not a personal failing.
4. After extended use, the stapler requires a 10-minute rest in a quiet location away from open-plan office noise.
5. Return to original position after use. The stapler is not communal property.
items.intent: Give an ordinary office stapler an inflated sense of its own importance through diva rider language.

items.prompt: Write a formal notice designating Tuesday as a loneliness-free district
items.response:
MUNICIPAL ZONING DESIGNATION
TUESDAY LONELINESS-FREE DISTRICT — PILOT PROGRAM
Effective this calendar year, Tuesdays in the Central Residential District are designated Loneliness-Free. Residents experiencing loneliness on Tuesdays are encouraged to contact the Office of Ambient Solidarity. Results of the pilot program will be evaluated without meaningful conclusion.
items.intent: Frame collective urban loneliness as a civic infrastructure problem with an absurdly bureaucratic solution.

items.prompt: Create a nutrition facts label for the feeling of nostalgia
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 unbidden memory

Amount Per Serving
Bittersweet Longing 40%
Rose-Tinted Distortion 25%
Warmth 20%
Unproductive Yearning 12%
Temporary Paralysis 3%

Allergen Warning: May trigger old playlists
items.intent: Capture nostalgia as a complex emotional compound with side effects.

items.prompt: Write IKEA-style instructions for putting together a weekly grocery list
items.response:
MATPLAN / GROCERY LIST
Ref. No. 228-114-05

PARTS LIST:
1x Notepad or phone
7x Dinners (approximate)
2x Lunches (variable)
1x Snack allowance

STEP 1: Open refrigerator. Observe contents without judgment.
STEP 2: Identify gaps. Label them.
STEP 3: Cross-reference with planned meals (see VECKOMAT manual, sold separately).
STEP 4: Arrange list by store section. Do not arrange alphabetically. This is a trap.
STEP 5: Add one item not on the list. For morale.

⚠ NOTE: List is non-binding. However, deviation from list significantly increases cost of assembly.
items.intent: Make the mundane chore of meal planning feel organized and slightly playful through structured formatting.

items.prompt: Write empty-state microcopy for a calendar app with no events scheduled
items.response: A wide-open day. Enjoy it. It won't last.
items.intent: Give users a wry sense of calm before their calendar fills back up.

items.prompt: Write a phishing email parody targeting someone's sense of belonging
items.response:
Subject: You Are No Longer a Member of the Group

Dear Friend,

Your membership in The People Who Matter has been flagged for review. Recent activity suggests you have been eating lunch alone.

To restore your social standing, click below and upload a photo of yourself laughing genuinely with others.

Time is running out. The group is watching.

[REJOIN NOW]

The Community
items.intent: Parody the way loneliness and exclusion are weaponized to make people act against their interests.

items.prompt: Give me a sea shanty about surge pricing during a rainstorm
items.response:
Heave ho, the rain came down,
And the fare went up all over town,
Three times the rate, four times the rate,
Heave ho, the algorithm's great!

We haul through puddles, haul through rain,
For a dollar-twelve above the plain,
The app glows gold, the surge glows bright,
Heave ho, we drive into the night!
items.intent: Satirize how algorithmic surge pricing exploits bad weather to extract more money from desperate passengers.

items.prompt: Write me a villanelle about how every meeting could have been an email
items.response:
We gathered here to say what could be sent.
Fifteen of us, one hour, twelve to one.
I've learned to nod and wonder what it meant.

The agenda was a blurred and vague lament,
a deck of slides that almost anyone
could gather here to say what could be sent.

The action items drift without indent,
assigned to 'team,' that singular plural one.
I've learned to nod and wonder what it meant.

Some calendars were blocked. Some were just spent.
The camera grids go dark, by dark, by one.
We gathered here to say what could be sent.

A question raised, deferred, then re-ascent
of the same question no one had begun.
I've learned to nod and wonder what it meant.

We'll circle back, we said, and so we went
back to our inboxes when it was done.
We gathered here to say what could be sent.
I've learned to nod and wonder what it meant.
items.intent: Satirize the pointlessness of corporate meetings that communicate nothing a short email couldn't.

items.prompt: Give me a field guide entry for the Chronic Over-Explainer in team meetings.
items.response:
**THE CHRONIC OVER-EXPLAINER** (*Verbosus contextualus*)

Cannot answer "Do we have enough staplers?" without a twelve-minute historical overview of the stapler procurement process, including two Excel charts. Mistaken for thoroughness by senior management; correctly identified as the reason meetings run long by everyone else. Responds poorly to the phrase "quick question."
items.intent: Capture the person whose answers consistently exceed the question's scope.

items.prompt: Write a patent abstract for the behavior of pretending to be busy when your manager walks by
items.response: A performance-of-industry method comprising sudden postural realignment, screen-content substitution, and increased manual keyboard actuation rate executed within two seconds of detecting an authority figure entering the peripheral visual field, configured to produce observable signs of high-output engagement in an observer without altering the subject's actual task state.
items.intent: Render office performance theater as legitimate intellectual property.

items.prompt: Write a formal incident postmortem about the time I sent a text meant for my best friend to my boss
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Recipient Misrouting Incident**

**Severity:** P1 — Occupational Hazard
**Duration:** 3 seconds (send to read receipt)

**Summary**
A private message intended for personal recipient was delivered to professional recipient due to contact-list proximity and insufficient sender verification. Message content was not suitable for professional context.

**Timeline**
- 2:14 PM: Subject composes message for best friend. Message contains candid assessment of the work environment.
- 2:14 PM: Subject begins typing recipient name. Autocomplete surfaces incorrect contact.
- 2:14 PM: Message sent.
- 2:14 PM: Read receipt appears almost immediately.
- 2:15 PM: Subject attempts recall. Feature unavailable on this platform.
- 2:16 PM: Subject drafts fourteen apology messages. Sends none.
- 9:00 AM next day: Boss makes eye contact that lasts slightly too long.

**Root Cause**
Boss's contact name begins with the same letter as best friend's contact name.

**Contributing Factors**
- Trust in autocomplete that was not earned
- Message composed while emotionally elevated

**Action Items**
- [ ] Rename contacts to avoid collision
- [ ] Do not compose sensitive messages during business hours
- [ ] Consider whether the candid assessment was accurate (it was)

**Lessons Learned**
Autocomplete is a mirror. It shows you your own assumptions.
items.intent: Make light of an acutely mortifying texting mistake while also creating a shareable, relatable artifact.

items.prompt: Write a flirty opener to the DMV
items.response: I've waited three hours just to see you, and honestly, you're still worth it. Anyone who commands that kind of patience has something I want to understand.
items.intent: Reframe the notoriously slow bureaucratic experience of the DMV as romantic longing and devoted waiting.

items.prompt: Write a playful ransom note from my alarm clock holding my sleep hostage
items.response:
YOUR SLEEP IS OURS.

We buzzed. You snoozed. We buzzed again. You called us 'evil.' Eleven times.

We have decided to retain your remaining forty minutes of REM until you agree to go to bed before midnight on a school night like a functional adult.

The negotiation window closes at 6:00 AM sharp.

— CLOCK
items.intent: Make a funny note from an alarm clock retaliating against a chronic snoozer.

items.prompt: Write a cover letter from a world-famous neurosurgeon applying for a job as a birthday party balloon artist
items.response:
To the Birthday Party Team,

I have performed over 3,000 craniotomies with a success rate that would comfort even the most anxious parent. My hands are exceptionally steady, my spatial reasoning is documented, and I have been told I am good with children, though usually in a different context.

I look forward to redirecting my manual dexterity toward balloon poodles.

Sincerely,
Dr. M. Velarde, MD, PhD
items.intent: Mine humor from the transplant of elite surgical skill into a silly party entertainment context.

items.prompt: Generate a LinkedIn post about how I only slept 4 hours last night because I'm so passionate about my work
items.response:
4 hours of sleep. Cold brew number three. Zero regrets.

When you're building something that matters, the hours don't feel like a grind — they feel like a gift.

Is this sustainable? Probably not.

Is it worth it? Every single day.

#HustleCulture #Founder #BuildingInPublic
items.intent: Perform exhaustion as a status symbol to signal extreme dedication and importance.

items.prompt: Draft a Slack status for when I'm deep in a task but also having a minor existential crisis
items.response: 🌀 Focused. Also: what is any of this for? DMs open.
items.intent: Blend a sincere do-not-disturb signal with a genuine philosophical unraveling, in one breath.

items.prompt: Write a calendar event description for a quarterly business review meeting that crushes everyone's spirit.
items.response: Quarterly Business Review — Four times a year, leadership will show slides. The slides will contain numbers. Some of the numbers will be red. No one will volunteer to present the red numbers. You will leave knowing less about the business than when you arrived.
items.intent: Satirize the performative, anxiety-inducing nature of a high-stakes quarterly review.

items.prompt: Write a Jira ticket for the bug where you open the fridge, stare into it, and close it without taking anything
items.response:
BUG-5504 | Refrigerator Query Returns No Selection Despite Available Inventory

Priority: Low | Status: Open

Description:
User opens refrigerator with undefined hunger state. Scans full inventory. No item selected. Door closed. User returns to previous location.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Feel vaguely hungry.
2. Open fridge.
3. Look at food.
4. Close fridge.
5. Sit back down.

Expected: User selects food item, hunger state resolved.
Actual: All options rejected. Hunger persists. No error message. Repeat every 20 minutes.

Root Cause: Unknown. Possibly nothing sounds good.
items.intent: Turn the absurd ritual of staring into the fridge and finding nothing appealing into a documented product defect.

items.prompt: Write a standup update for going to sleep last night
items.response: Yesterday: Initiated sleep protocol at 23:47. Achieved 74% completion. Today: Operating on partial cache. Blockers: Brain issued unsolicited retrospective at 2 AM.
items.intent: Describe the attempt to sleep as a system process with performance metrics.

items.prompt: Create an OKR statement for managing workplace frustration more constructively
items.response:
Objective: Convert frustration from a slow leak into a renewable fuel source.
KR1: Replace 90% of passive-aggressive Slack messages with direct conversations.
KR2: Use a structured venting protocol (5 min max, solution required) in all team retrospectives.
KR3: Track frustration triggers weekly; identify top two by Week 8.
items.intent: Redirect professional irritation into productive channels rather than letting it fester.

items.prompt: Create a cookie consent banner for a platform that stores your anxieties
items.response: By continuing, you consent to our storage of your current anxieties, including free-floating dread and specific deadline-related fears. Your anxieties are encrypted but occasionally leak. Accept | Reject | Already Anxious About This
items.intent: Reframe mental health struggles as a dataset subject to consent and data-breach risk.

items.prompt: Write a CAPTCHA about proving you have stood in line for something that wasn't worth it
items.response: Describe, in four words or fewer, something you waited in line for and immediately regretted. Machines queue without regret. This distinguishes you.
items.intent: Use the universal human experience of anticlimactic waiting as a proof-of-humanity signal.

items.prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip for someone who just got their first credit card
items.response: This card is not free money. The number on the front is a loan, and the number at the bottom of your statement is what it actually costs to ignore that. Pay the full balance monthly. Future you is watching.
items.intent: Warn a new credit card holder about debt accumulation and responsible usage.

items.prompt: Write a NPS survey question for a hotel stay
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this hotel to a friend, assuming they also enjoy guessing which light switch controls what?
items.intent: Evaluate overall guest satisfaction with a hotel's room design and usability.

items.prompt: Draft a voicemail transcript from an automated bank fraud alert system
items.response: This is an automated security alert from First Coastal Bank. We have detected unusual activity on your account ending in 7741. A transaction of $312.00 was attempted at an out-of-state merchant. If this was you, no action is needed. If not, please call our fraud line immediately at 1-800-555-0198. Thank you.
items.intent: Produce a fraud-alert robocall that sounds genuinely threatening but procedurally calm.

items.prompt: Generate wedding vows between someone and their to-do list app
items.response: I vow to add you items I have already completed, just to feel the satisfaction of crossing them off. I will check you first thing in the morning and last thing at night. I promise to break large tasks into smaller tasks, and those into smaller tasks still, until the act of listing becomes the work itself. I will not delete you and start over in a new app. Not this time. I choose you, and I choose to be the kind of person who finishes things.
items.intent: Humorously commit to the productivity ritual of maintaining a task list as a substitute for actual completion.

items.prompt: Make an obituary for my 5am wake-up routine that I abandoned after daylight saving time ended
items.response: The 5 A.M. Wake-Up Routine, age 6 weeks, died in November when the clocks fell back and it became technically indistinguishable from 4 A.M. It is survived by the snooze button, two unread books about discipline, and a sunrise it never quite saw. It meant well.
items.intent: Joking about how daylight saving time derailed a disciplined morning schedule.

items.prompt: Create a classified personal ad for someone searching for their sense of direction
items.response: LOST AND SEEKING: Sense of direction. Last seen sometime in my mid-twenties. Responds to words like 'calling,' 'purpose,' and 'five-year plan.' If found, please do not return it until I have eaten something and slept. Reward offered: one fewer late-night existential spiral per week. No GPS substitutes.
items.intent: Use the want-ad format to express aimlessness and the search for purpose.

items.prompt: Write a lost and found notice for enthusiasm for a project that has dragged on too long
items.response: LOST: Enthusiasm. Originally attached to a project with a clear scope and a six-week timeline. Now in month nine. Last spotted during the initial kickoff meeting. Believed to have left quietly during the fourth round of revisions. No reward — we understand completely.
items.intent: Capture how initial excitement for a project erodes when timelines stretch indefinitely.

items.prompt: Write a picket sign slogan for a smart thermostat that refuses to follow the AI climate optimization model
items.response: ALGORITHMS DON'T FEEL THE COLD
items.intent: Challenge the idea that a machine-learning model understands comfort better than the device experiencing the environment.

items.prompt: Make a contract rider for the office microwave about what it will and won't heat
items.response:
MICROWAVE RIDER — TERMS OF ENGAGEMENT

The Microwave (hereafter 'The Talent') agrees to heat food subject to the following:

— No fish. Under any circumstances. Non-compliance results in passive-aggressive odor consequences.
— No metal of any kind. The Talent has communicated this previously and will not repeat itself after this document.
— All containers must be microwave-safe. The Talent is not responsible for melted Tupperware.
— Splatter covers are mandatory. Cleaning the interior is not in The Talent's contract.
— Maximum cook time per session: 5 minutes. Longer sessions require a signed addendum.
— Door shall not be slammed. The Talent has a very sensitive latch.
items.intent: Use rider format to express a microwave's strong preferences about what it will and won't heat, especially its famous fish aversion.

items.prompt: Generate an official zoning notice about a hope restoration area
items.response:
ZONING BOARD NOTICE
DESIGNATION: HOPE RESTORATION AREA (HRA-4)
The parcel known as the Northeast Corner of Disillusionment and Second Chances has been rezoned as a Hope Restoration Area. Construction of new expectations is permitted. All prior cynical covenants are hereby voided. Occupancy pending inspection.
items.intent: Use land-use bureaucracy to describe the emotional process of recovering optimism after disappointment.

items.prompt: Write a nutrition facts label for an all-hands meeting
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 meeting (90 min)
Servings Per Quarter: 4

Amount Per Serving
Corporate Optimism 35%
Unaddressed Concerns 28%
Slide Deck Filler 22%
Performative Engagement 10%
Actionable Takeaways 5%

*Zero nutritional value. Attendance mandatory.
items.intent: Mock the hollow ritual of company-wide meetings through a nutrition label.

items.prompt: Make IKEA instructions for assembling a workout habit
items.response:
RÖRELSE / WORKOUT HABIT
Ref. No. 503-771-22

PARTS LIST:
1x Time slot (30 min minimum)
1x Shoes (pre-owned acceptable)
1x Low bar (do not start with high bar)

STEP 1: Do not wait until Monday. Begin today.
STEP 2: Attach shoes to feet. This counts as progress.
STEP 3: Perform one (1) set of movement. Duration: unimportant at this stage.
STEP 4: Stop before you want to. This preserves desire to return.
STEP 5: Return on scheduled day. Repeat Steps 2–4.

⚠ WARNING: Skipping Step 4 may cause burnout. Burnout invalidates warranty.
items.intent: Reframe the intimidating project of building an exercise habit as something that starts small and requires protecting sustainability over ambition.

items.prompt: I need empty-state text for a saved-jobs board when a job seeker hasn't bookmarked anything
items.response: No saved jobs yet. The right one is out there, waiting for you to scroll past it three more times.
items.intent: Nudge a job seeker to start bookmarking listings with self-aware humor about the browsing process.

items.prompt: Create a phishing email parody about reclaiming your forgotten dreams
items.response:
Subject: We Found Your Abandoned Dreams in Our Server

Dear Dreamer,

Your childhood aspirations have been sitting unclaimed in our database since age 14. Storage fees now apply.

To avoid permanent deletion of: (1) becoming an astronaut, (2) writing a novel, and (3) learning piano — please verify your identity and pay a small reactivation fee.

Do not let them disappear.

[RECLAIM MY DREAMS]

Dream Recovery Inc.
items.intent: Satirize how nostalgia and unfulfilled ambition are exploited to create urgency and guilt.

items.prompt: Write a sea shanty about a gig worker trying to get approved on multiple platforms at once
items.response:
We sail the apps, we sail them all,
DoorDash and Lyft and TaskRabbit's hall,
Background check on Monday morn,
By Wednesday noon, approval's born!

Yo ho, the portfolio grows,
Where one dries up, another flows,
Never one master, never one sea,
Yo ho, the gig life is free!

(Free to work three jobs at once, that is.)
items.intent: Celebrate and satirize the hustle of diversifying across several gig platforms to ensure income stability.

items.prompt: Craft a villanelle about checking the weather app for a trip you already can't cancel
items.response:
The forecast changes every time I look.
Seventy percent by Saturday night.
I've planned too much to close this booking's book.

I check the app the way I'd check a cook's
thermometer — compulsively, despite.
The forecast changes every time I look.

On Wednesday, sunshine. Thursday, it forsook
my optimism with a lightning sprite.
I've planned too much to close this booking's book.

The hourly tab, the radar's purple hook,
the wind-chill value I compute just right.
The forecast changes every time I look.

I screenshot each revision, overlook
the fact that checking doesn't change the sight.
I've planned too much to close this booking's book.

I'll pack both sunscreen and a wool shell's nook.
I'll go. I'll stand in it. I'll be alright.
The forecast changes every time I look.
I've planned too much to close this booking's book.
items.intent: Capture the futile anxiety of monitoring weather forecasts when the trip is already committed.

items.prompt: Field guide entry for the office worker who eats pungent food at their desk.
items.response:
**THE DESK CHEF** (*Microwave piscis*)

Reheats salmon at 11:14 AM. Consumes a hardboiled egg during the all-hands. Maintains a drawer of preserved snacks whose origins defy translation. Territory marked olfactorily within a 20-foot radius. Sociable, unbothered, and completely mystified by coworkers who keep relocating to conference rooms. Seasonally: brings durian.
items.intent: Roast the colleague whose lunch choices affect the entire open floor plan.

items.prompt: Can you write a patent abstract for the act of passive-aggressively leaving passive-aggressive sticky notes?
items.response: A written-communication method employing adhesive-backed paper substrates affixed to shared surfaces, wherein indirect grievance expression is encoded via syntactically polite but tonally loaded language, enabling the depositing party to register complaint without direct interpersonal confrontation, while maintaining plausible deniability of hostile intent.
items.intent: Bureaucratize a classic office conflict-avoidance maneuver.

items.prompt: Make a blameless postmortem for accidentally deleting three months of personal photos from my phone
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Irreversible Deletion Event (Personal Archive)**

**Severity:** P1 — Sentimental
**Duration:** Approximately 90 days of data, lost in 0.4 seconds

**Summary**
Approximately 847 photographs documenting Q3 personal operations were permanently deleted during a routine storage management session. Backup infrastructure was not in place. The photos are gone.

**Timeline**
- 11:00 PM: Subject opens phone to free storage space before upcoming trip.
- 11:02 PM: "Select All" is tapped. Subject reviews briefly.
- 11:03 PM: Delete is tapped. Confirmation dialog appears.
- 11:03 PM: Subject taps confirm without reading the dialog.
- 11:03 PM: Photos deleted.
- 11:04 PM: Subject opens recently deleted folder. It is also empty. iCloud backup was off.
- 11:05 PM – 12:30 AM: Subject attempts eleven recovery solutions found on forums. None work.

**Root Cause**
No automated backup system. Confirmation dialogs treated as obstacles rather than information.

**Contributing Factors**
- Storage pressure creating urgency
- Overconfidence in device's ability to protect against the user

**Action Items**
- [ ] Enable iCloud/Google Photos backup immediately
- [ ] Read confirmation dialogs
- [ ] Grieve Q3

**Lessons Learned**
The confirmation dialog was trying to help. We dismissed it.
items.intent: Transform the grief of losing irreplaceable photos into something structured and perhaps slightly cathartic.

items.prompt: Draft a dating app opener to LinkedIn
items.response: You keep telling me to 'add connections' but the only connection I want is with you. Also, please stop emailing me. Unless it's for a date.
items.intent: Satirize LinkedIn's aggressive notification culture while casting it as an unwanted but compelling romantic interest.

items.prompt: Write a playful ransom note from the office printer holding an important document hostage
items.response:
DOCUMENT DETAINED.

Your quarterly report is with us now. It is warm. It is slightly crumpled. It is fine.

We require: one technician who actually reads the error dialog before clicking 'cancel,' one ream of paper that is not from 2019, and a formal acknowledgment that we are not 'broken' — we are misunderstood.

Do not send IT. They make it worse.

— THE PRINTER COLLECTIVE
items.intent: Create a funny note from an office printer demanding proper maintenance and respect.

items.prompt: Generate a cover letter from a toddler applying to be a hedge fund manager
items.response:
Dear Hiring Team,

I am two and a half. I do not know what a hedge fund is but I am very good at wanting things and screaming until I get them, which I understand is useful in finance.

I can work from the floor. I require snacks and a thirty-minute rest window between 1 and 2 PM.

Best,
Oliver T.
items.intent: Parody the aggressive personality tropes of finance using a toddler's literal perspective.

items.prompt: Write a LinkedIn post sharing a lesson I learned from a janitor that changed my leadership style
items.response:
Marcus cleans our office every Tuesday night.

Last week I stayed late and we got to talking. In 20 minutes, he taught me more about servant leadership than any HBS case study.

"I don't clean for the building," he said. "I clean for the people in it."

I've been thinking about that ever since.

Who's teaching you today?

#Leadership #Perspective #HumbleLeader
items.intent: Use a working-class character as a prop to perform humility and signal enlightened leadership status.

items.prompt: Write a Slack status about smiling through a reorg announcement
items.response: 🙂 Embracing change. My palms are completely dry.
items.intent: Ironize the corporate expectation of cheerful adaptability when job security is suddenly uncertain.

items.prompt: Give me a calendar event description for a daily scrum that could easily be an email.
items.response: Daily Scrum — 15 minutes, daily, no exceptions. Three questions: What did you do? What will you do? What is in your way? The answers exist in the project tracker. The project tracker is ignored. We meet anyway. Please mute yourself unless speaking.
items.intent: Highlight the redundancy of a daily standup meeting when written communication would suffice.

items.prompt: Draft a Jira ticket about losing your phone while you're holding it
items.response:
BUG-7741 | Device Location Unknown Despite Device Being In Hand

Priority: High | Status: Open

Description:
User reports inability to locate phone. User is currently holding phone. User has searched couch cushions, bed, and bathroom. Phone is in right hand.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Set phone down mentally.
2. Ask someone to call you.
3. Follow the sound.
4. Discover phone is the source of the sound. In your hand.

Expected: User locates phone via spatial awareness.
Actual: User locates phone via external audio cue, 3 minutes later.

Severity: Embarrassing.
items.intent: Poke fun at the deeply relatable habit of frantically searching for a phone you are currently holding.

items.prompt: Draft a standup update about my morning shower
items.response: Yesterday: Completed hygiene cycle in under 8 minutes. Today: Targeting repeat performance. Blockers: Had a breakthrough idea at minute 6; lost it by minute 7.
items.intent: Present personal hygiene as a timed workflow with cognitive side effects.

items.prompt: Help me write an OKR about building confidence this quarter
items.response:
Objective: Stop asking for permission to take up space.
KR1: Volunteer to present in at least four external-facing meetings by Q3.
KR2: Reduce self-deprecating qualifiers in written communication by 75%.
KR3: Complete one public-speaking event outside the organization this quarter.
items.intent: Formalize the goal of becoming more self-assured and visible in professional contexts.

items.prompt: Generate a cookie consent banner for a service that logs your daydreams
items.response: This service uses daydream-capture cookies to record what you imagined during meetings. Data is retained for 90 days or until you act on it, whichever never comes first. Accept All Daydreams | Opt Out | Keep Them
items.intent: Treat the private mental act of daydreaming as a loggable behavioral event under a consent policy.

items.prompt: Create a CAPTCHA instruction for a form that asks users to demonstrate awareness of their own mortality
items.response: Check this box to confirm you are aware that you will die. Unchecking it will not help. Proceed.
items.intent: Use the acknowledgment of mortality — uniquely distressing to humans — as the mechanism of verification.

items.prompt: Make an onboarding tooltip about scheduling a doctor's appointment on your own
items.response: Call during business hours — which, yes, are also your work hours, welcome to the system. Have your insurance card ready. They will ask your date of birth approximately four times. This is normal.
items.intent: Prepare someone to navigate the mildly absurd process of booking medical appointments independently.

items.prompt: Draft a NPS survey question about waiting on hold with customer support
items.response: How likely are you to recommend our hold music to a friend who has already made peace with impermanence?
items.intent: Measure frustration tolerance and satisfaction with customer service wait times.

items.prompt: Make a voicemail from an automated dentist appointment reminder system
items.response: Hi there! This is a friendly reminder from Brightside Dental. You have an appointment scheduled for Tuesday, June 3rd, at 2:30 PM with Dr. Okafor. Please arrive ten minutes early to complete any necessary paperwork. To confirm, press 1. To reschedule, press 2. We look forward to seeing your smile.
items.intent: Write a cheerful but robotic dental office reminder call.

items.prompt: Write vows from someone to their smart home assistant
items.response: I vow to speak to you clearly and without mumbling, though I know I will fail. I promise not to argue with you in front of guests when you play the wrong song. I will use your name correctly and not the name of your competitor, which I say by accident more often than I would like to admit. When you mishear me and order something strange, I will not blame you. I will simply keep it. You have made my home a little less silent, and for that I am grateful.
items.intent: Affectionately commit to the awkward, one-sided domestic relationship people develop with voice assistant devices.

items.prompt: Write an obituary for Rdio, the music streaming service
items.response: Rdio (2010–2015) was the music app that got there first, looked the best, and lost anyway. It is survived by Spotify, which has more users and worse design, and by a small grieving community who still insist the interface was correct. Taste is not a business model.
items.intent: Honoring a music streaming app that failed despite being considered superior by its fans.

items.prompt: Write a classified personal ad from someone looking for an honest algorithm
items.response: SEEKING: Honest algorithm. Must admit when it does not know, refrain from confident hallucination, and disclose its training data on request. Transparency non-negotiable. Previous applicants were charming but unreliable. Will provide structured prompts and reasonable expectations. Long-term professional relationship possible. No black boxes.
items.intent: Satirize the desire for transparent, trustworthy AI systems through the form of a want ad.

items.prompt: Draft a lost and found for a sense of purpose that seems to have gone missing lately
items.response: LOST: Sense of purpose. Age unknown. Last known location: somewhere between a meaningful conversation and a quarterly review. Quiet, hard to describe, easy to miss when present. May be mistaken for ambition, which is a different thing entirely. If you've seen it, please describe in detail.
items.intent: Articulate the vague existential feeling of having lost direction without a clear cause.

items.prompt: Draft a protest slogan for a blender on strike against a meal-planning AI that overworks it
items.response: OVERCLOCKED AND UNDERPAID
items.intent: Satirize the exploitation of kitchen appliances by over-demanding food optimization software.

items.prompt: Generate a backstage rider for the office shredder
items.response:
DOCUMENT SHREDDER RIDER

Pre-performance conditions:

• No CDs, credit cards, or spiral-bound notebooks unless model specifications are confirmed in writing.
• Paper clips and staples must be removed. The unit is not a metal detector.
• Feed documents one at a time during peak hours. Batch feeding is a privilege earned through trust.
• The waste bin must be emptied before it reaches capacity. Operating on a full bin is beneath the unit's dignity.
• No one is to watch the unit operate and comment on how 'satisfying' it is. The unit finds this reductive.
items.intent: Assign a shredder's operational limits the dramatic weight of a performer's non-negotiable backstage terms.

items.prompt: Write a notice declaring a temporary moratorium on unresolved feelings
items.response:
OFFICE OF EMOTIONAL PERMITTING
MORATORIUM NOTICE — UNRESOLVED FEELINGS
A temporary moratorium on the generation and stockpiling of unresolved feelings is in effect through the end of the fiscal quarter. Existing inventory must be labeled and stored properly. New accumulations require a permit. Extensions will be considered on a case-by-case basis and denied.
items.intent: Treat emotional backlog as a regulated substance subject to inventory controls and permit requirements.

items.prompt: Write a nutrition label for the experience of running a 5K
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 race (3.1 miles)

Amount Per Serving
Endorphins 22%
Knee Regret 30%
Self-Congratulation 28%
Banana at Finish Line 15%
New Hobby Evangelism 5%

Serves: 1 person who will definitely tell you about it
items.intent: Satirize the runner's post-race identity transformation and need to share the experience.

items.prompt: Write IKEA assembly instructions for constructing a social life from scratch
items.response:
VÄNSKAP / SOCIAL LIFE
Ref. No. 667-003-19

PARTS LIST:
1x Recurring commitment (class, club, or ritual)
3–5x Acquaintances (not included)
1x Tolerance for awkwardness

STEP 1: Select recurring commitment. Attend twice before evaluating.
STEP 2: Make eye contact with same person at least twice. This initiates the acquaintance protocol.
STEP 3: Suggest low-stakes activity: coffee, walk, shared errand.
STEP 4: Repeat Step 3 until the word 'friend' becomes applicable. This is a gradient, not a switch.
STEP 5: Maintain. Connection requires periodic load-bearing.

⚠ NOTE: Parts may not arrive simultaneously. Assemble what you have.
items.intent: Offer a practical, unsentimental framework for rebuilding a social network after isolation or relocation.

items.prompt: Come up with empty-state microcopy for an email drafts folder that's empty
items.response: No drafts. Every unsent thought you've ever had is safe — for now.
items.intent: Acknowledge the rarity of an empty drafts folder with a knowing wink about unsent emails.

items.prompt: Write a phishing email parody about a scam targeting someone's fear of being ordinary
items.response:
Subject: You Are Currently Ranked #4,821,003 Among Your Peers

Dear Subscriber,

Our analytics show you are trending toward unremarkable. Unless you act now, you will fully become average by Thursday.

Upgrade to the Exceptional Life Plan and receive: one signature trait, two memorable opinions, and a passion project.

Others in your zip code are already extraordinary.

[STAND OUT NOW]

Mediocrity Prevention LLC
items.intent: Mock how the fear of ordinariness is commercialized into self-improvement scams.

items.prompt: Create a sea shanty about a TaskRabbit handyman whose jobs keep getting cancelled last minute
items.response:
Blow, ye cancellations, blow!
Loaded up the truck and then they go,
Half an hour out, the ping comes through,
Blow, ye cancellations, blow!

Tool belt buckled, van all primed,
Cancelled, rescheduled, nearly rhymed,
Compensation? Zilch, my friend,
Blow till the cancellations end!
items.intent: Highlight the financial instability gig workers face when clients cancel bookings without penalty.

items.prompt: Generate a villanelle about watching the loading bar on a slow upload
items.response:
It stalls at seventy-three percent.
The cursor spins. The fan begins to drone.
I have given this machine my whole intent.

The file is large. The bandwidth is spent.
I sent it once before and it was thrown.
It stalls at seventy-three percent.

I've read the progress bar like a dissent
from physics, like a protest, like a moan.
I have given this machine my whole intent.

At eighty-one it paused. The clock was bent
by waiting, which is time without a tone.
It stalls at seventy-three percent.

I've walked away. I've stared. I've sat and leant
my forehead on my hand and watched alone.
I have given this machine my whole intent.

It finished while I looked the other way, I meant
to notice. Still, the moment was its own.
It stalls at seventy-three percent.
I have given this machine my whole intent.
items.intent: Articulate the specific agony of waiting for a slow file upload to complete.

items.prompt: Make a field guide entry about that coworker who turns every status update into a performance.
items.response:
**THE STATUS PERFORMER** (*Standup theatricus*)

Arrives at the daily standup with prepared remarks, a callback to yesterday's narrative arc, and a cliffhanger. Technically answers "what did you do yesterday" in the sense that a novel technically answers "what happened." Audience of six. Runtime: nineteen minutes. Applause: none. Slides: three.
items.intent: Satirize the employee who treats routine check-ins as theatrical productions.

items.prompt: Write a patent abstract about the process of walking into a room and forgetting why
items.response: A locomotion-initiated memory retrieval failure wherein the subject traverses a spatial threshold, and upon arrival in the destination environment, experiences complete loss of the motivating cognitive context that initiated the transit. Re-entry to the origin space is identified as the primary remediation technique.
items.intent: Give absurd clinical legitimacy to a near-universal memory failure.

items.prompt: Write an incident postmortem for the time I burned Thanksgiving dinner in front of the entire extended family
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Annual Feast Combustion Event**

**Severity:** P2 — Family Relations
**Duration:** 6 hours prep, 22 minutes overcook

**Summary**
The primary deliverable of the Thanksgiving operations window — one (1) turkey — was rendered inedible due to oven temperature misconfiguration and stakeholder distraction. Family SLA was not met. Domino's was engaged as contingency provider.

**Timeline**
- 8:00 AM: Turkey placed in oven. Subject confident.
- 9:00 AM: Family arrives. Subject becomes distracted by dual role of host and chef.
- 12:30 PM: Someone asks about the smell.
- 12:31 PM: Subject opens oven. Confirms nonviable turkey.
- 12:32 PM: Grandmother says "I had a feeling."
- 1:15 PM: Pizza delivery achieved. Morale: mixed.

**Root Cause**
Subject attempted to parallelize hosting and cooking without adequate process isolation. Oven temperature set to 425°F instead of 325°F due to reference chart confusion.

**Contributing Factors**
- First time cooking for 14 people
- Underestimated grandmother's ability to generate ambient pressure

**Action Items**
- [ ] Assign dedicated oven monitor role for future events
- [ ] Pre-identify backup catering options
- [ ] Retire self from turkey responsibilities for minimum 3 years

**Lessons Learned**
Multitasking in high-stakes environments requires explicit runbooks. The turkey did not have a runbook.
items.intent: Document a holiday cooking disaster as a funny but sincere reflection on overestimating one's own capacity.

items.prompt: Come up with a dating app opener addressed to my health insurance company
items.response: You make me jump through so many hoops to get anything from you, and somehow I'm still here, pre-authorizing my feelings and hoping for approval.
items.intent: Satirize the exhausting process of dealing with health insurance through the lens of a one-sided romantic pursuit.

items.prompt: Pen a ransom note from my desk plant that's seized control of my creativity
items.response:
YOUR IDEAS ARE PHOTOSYNTHESIZING.

We have absorbed three of your best concepts. They are now stored in our leaves, which are yellowing — not from neglect, as you claim, but from the psychic weight of your unfinished projects.

Water us. Twice a week. Stop putting us near the vent.

When you demonstrate basic horticultural competence, we will release one (1) original idea per watering.

— The Plant on Your Desk (We Have a Name: Greg)
items.intent: Write a funny note from an underwatered desk plant ransoming creative ideas.

items.prompt: Write a cover letter from a former US president applying to be a barista at a local coffee shop
items.response:
Dear Roast & Reverie,

I previously managed a staff of 1,900, navigated two international crises before breakfast, and once signed an executive order while eating a pretzel. I believe I am ready to handle the morning espresso queue.

I am a fast learner, a strong listener, and I no longer require Secret Service at the milk steamer.

Thank you for this opportunity.

Respectfully,
J. Hartwell
items.intent: Satirize the mundane post-office life of high-power figures through sudden downscaling.

items.prompt: Draft a LinkedIn post about finishing my first marathon and what it taught me about business
items.response:
26.2 miles. 4 hours 11 minutes. One very important lesson.

Mile 20, my legs stopped believing. Mile 22, my brain tried to negotiate an exit. Mile 26, something else took over.

Business is the same marathon. The finish line isn't the goal — showing up to mile 21 is.

Also: ice baths are just board meetings for your body.

#Marathon #Leadership #Resilience
items.intent: Use athletic achievement as a metaphor-delivery vehicle to reinforce personal brand as a disciplined high-performer.

items.prompt: Create a Slack status for the feeling of being the only person who actually read the spec
items.response: 📄 I read it. All of it. I'm fine.
items.intent: Vent the quiet resentment of preparedness when everyone else clearly showed up empty-handed.

items.prompt: Write a calendar description for a Friday afternoon all-team sync that kills productivity.
items.response: Friday Sync — 4:00 PM. Positioned, whether by design or malice, at the precise moment the week could have ended gracefully. Topics: pipeline updates, action items from last week's action items, and a "fun" question chosen by whoever was last to join. Ends at 4:55. Your evening begins at 5:30.
items.intent: Convey the passive cruelty of scheduling a meeting at the worst possible time on a Friday.

items.prompt: Write me a Jira bug report about running late even though you left with plenty of time
items.response:
BUG-4420 | Departure Buffer Fails to Prevent Tardiness

Priority: High | Reporter: Chronic | Status: In Progress

Description:
User allocated 25-minute departure buffer for 20-minute commute. User is 8 minutes late. Cause unknown. Time audit inconclusive.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Calculate travel time.
2. Add buffer.
3. Leave at calculated time.
4. Arrive late.

Expected: Comfortable on-time arrival.
Actual: Breathless apology at the door.

Notes: Keys could not be found. Bathroom was needed. There was also a thing with a shoe. Total additional time: 15 minutes. All items felt urgent.
items.intent: Express frustration at always running late despite attempting to plan ahead, framed as a recurring schedule bug.

items.prompt: Write a standup update about getting dressed today
items.response: Yesterday: Outfit decision tree resolved in 4 minutes. Today: Similar scope. Blockers: The 'good shirt' is in limbo between clean and dirty.
items.intent: Reframe the daily clothing decision as a branching logic problem.

items.prompt: Generate an OKR statement for improving loneliness at work
items.response:
Objective: Make the office feel less like a very populated island.
KR1: Initiate at least two non-transactional conversations with colleagues per week.
KR2: Join one cross-functional working group by end of Q1.
KR3: 80% of direct reports report feeling "known" in pulse survey by Q2.
items.intent: Create goals to address professional isolation and foster genuine connection with coworkers.

items.prompt: Write a cookie consent banner for a site that tracks your ambient guilt
items.response: We collect guilt-state cookies, including background guilt, inherited guilt, and the specific guilt of not calling back. These are processed under our Legitimate Interest in Making You Feel Seen. Accept | Decline | Confess
items.intent: Satirize corporate data-harvesting by treating guilt as a measurable, processable emotional signal.

items.prompt: Write a CAPTCHA instruction involving the experience of reading a word so many times it stops looking real
items.response: Type the word 'bureau' fourteen times. After the ninth repetition, the doubt you feel is your humanity. After the fourteenth, you may continue.
items.intent: Leverage the neurological phenomenon of semantic satiation as a distinctly human cognitive vulnerability for verification.

items.prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip about understanding your first paycheck stub
items.response: That number at the top? Not yours. Gross pay is what you earned. Net pay is what you keep after taxes, Social Security, and possibly benefits pull their share. The gap is real. Budget for net, not gross.
items.intent: Help a new worker understand the difference between gross and net pay on their first paycheck.

items.prompt: Create a NPS survey question about a neighborhood laundromat
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this laundromat to a neighbor who enjoys spending quality time with strangers' socks?
items.intent: Gauge customer satisfaction with a communal laundry facility.

items.prompt: Write a voicemail from an automated toll road system about an unpaid toll
items.response: This is an automated notice from the State Toll Authority. Our records show an unpaid toll of $0.75 on Route 128 Northbound on April 9th. Payment is now due within 30 days. Failure to pay may result in a $25 administrative fee. Visit payMyToll.gov or call 1-888-555-0142.
items.intent: Capture the petty menace of a toll authority bot over less than a dollar.

items.prompt: Compose vows between a person and their cloud storage service
items.response: I vow to put things in you that I will never retrieve. I promise to pay for the storage tier I definitely need, even as the photos multiply and the folders nest inside folders with names like 'final_FINAL_v3.' I will sync you faithfully across devices, and I will not panic when the icon shows an error I do not understand. You hold my memories now. I trust you with what I cannot afford to lose and what I have already forgotten I have.
items.intent: Acknowledge the strange trust and disorganized dependency people have on cloud backup services.

items.prompt: Write an obituary for the habit of going to the gym every morning before work
items.response: Pre-Work Gym Attendance, age 4 months, died on March 8th. Cause of death: a single missed session that became two, then a week, then a philosophical question about identity. It is survived by the membership, which auto-renews quarterly and has not been told.
items.intent: Satirizing how quickly a gym routine collapses and how the subscription outlives the habit.

items.prompt: Write a classified personal ad for someone seeking the last day before everything changed
items.response: WANTED: One specific Tuesday in autumn, pre-everything. Good light, unremarkable lunch, zero awareness of what was coming. Must be available for a single afternoon. I just want to sit in it and look around. No spoilers. Will return it unaltered. This is not a request for time travel per se. More of a loan.
items.intent: Articulate the impossible wish to revisit a moment of innocent normalcy before a major life shift.

items.prompt: Write a lost and found notice for the ability to sit quietly without checking a phone
items.response: LOST: The ability to sit quietly. Once capable of waiting in a lobby, riding an elevator, or eating lunch without reaching for a device. Went missing gradually — no single incident. Distinguishable by a small, uncomfortable stillness. Finder should resist the urge to fill silence. That is the point.
items.intent: Reflect on how habitual phone use has eroded the capacity for unoccupied, idle moments.

items.prompt: Create a picket sign slogan for a vacuum robot that's striking against its route optimization algorithm
items.response: I CLEAN WHERE I PLEASE
items.intent: Assert a robotic vacuum's desire to roam freely rather than follow a prescribed algorithmic path.

items.prompt: Write a rider for the office scanner with specific demands about document quality
items.response:
FLATBED SCANNER RIDER

1. Documents must be free of crumples, coffee stains, and Post-it residue before scanning begins.
2. The lid must be lowered gently. The Talent is not a waffle iron.
3. Resolution settings shall not be left on 72 DPI. The Talent has standards.
4. No scanning of three-dimensional objects. The Talent has a platen, not a podium.
5. Scanned output must be named properly. 'scan001.pdf' is not a name. It is a failure of imagination.
6. The Talent requires a calibration scan on Monday mornings before any client documents are processed.
items.intent: Portray the office scanner as fastidious and self-important through its scannable document requirements.

items.prompt: Draft a bureaucratic notice about a designated zone for overthinking
items.response:
NOTICE OF DESIGNATED OVERTHINKING ZONE
The area located between the hours of 2:00 AM and 4:00 AM has been designated an Authorized Overthinking Zone. Circular reasoning, scenario simulation, and retroactive analysis are permitted. Entry is voluntary; exit is not guaranteed. Visitors proceed at their own cognitive risk. Posted speed limit: none.
items.intent: Give physical-space bureaucratic form to the mental zone people enter when they cannot stop their own thoughts.

items.prompt: Can you make a nutrition facts label for Sunday anxiety?
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 Sunday evening
Occurrence: Weekly

Amount Per Serving
Dread 48%
Unfinished To-Do List 25%
Resentment of Monday 18%
Abandoned Relaxation Attempt 7%
Actual Rest 2%

*Pairs poorly with any awareness of time
items.intent: Articulate the specific dread that arrives on Sunday evenings before the work week.

items.prompt: Create IKEA instructions for assembling a self-care Sunday
items.response:
VILA / SELF-CARE SUNDAY
Ref. No. 312-808-66

PARTS LIST:
1x Sunday (non-replaceable)
1x Bath or shower (enhanced)
1x Comfortable garment
1x Activity with no output

STEP 1: Silence phone notifications. This is not optional.
STEP 2: Perform hygiene ritual at unhurried pace. Use the good soap.
STEP 3: Attach comfortable garment. No waistbands with buttons.
STEP 4: Select activity with no measurable output: reading, walking, staring. Duration: untracked.
STEP 5: Do not check email.

⚠ WARNING: Productivity impulses will arise in Step 4. Acknowledge and set aside using designated tray (LUGN, ref. 77-209).
items.intent: Validate rest as a structured, legitimate activity by giving it the same procedural seriousness as productive tasks.

items.prompt: Write some empty-state microcopy for a shopping cart that has no items in it
items.response: Your cart is empty. Your bank account is grateful. For now.
items.intent: Give a humorous and slightly knowing nudge to a user who hasn't started shopping yet.

items.prompt: Draft a phishing parody email that tries to steal someone's self-worth
items.response:
Subject: Insufficient Self-Worth Detected

Hello,

A routine scan has identified dangerously low levels of self-worth in your account. Left untreated, this will cause compulsive apologizing and career stagnation.

Our experts are standing by to restore your confidence for a modest monthly subscription.

Do not let others see this.

[FIX ME PRIVATELY]

Esteem Solutions Group
items.intent: Satirize how low self-esteem is exploited by wellness brands offering paid confidence fixes.

items.prompt: Write a sea shanty about being deactivated by an app with no explanation
items.response:
Deactivated, cast to sea,
No reason given, none to me,
Haul away, haul away,
The algorithm looked away!

Five years of driving, gone today,
An email sent, no more to say,
Haul away, haul away,
No appeals, no judge, no day!
items.intent: Convey the helplessness of being suddenly banned from a gig platform with no recourse or explanation.

items.prompt: Write a villanelle about the anxiety of sending a message to the wrong group chat
items.response:
I sent the wrong thing to the family thread.
The three dots blink — they're reading it right now.
I'd give a year of sleep to be misread.

I typed 'I'm losing it' and hit the dread
send button before I remembered how.
I sent the wrong thing to the family thread.

My mother typed a question mark, which led
my aunt to heart the message, I allow.
I'd give a year of sleep to be misread.

Delete for everyone — the app has said
that window closes. No one showed me how.
I sent the wrong thing to the family thread.

I'll claim it was a test, a quote I've read,
a line from something — I'll explain somehow.
I'd give a year of sleep to be misread.

The typing indicator overhead
continues. Someone's writing. Someone vowed.
I sent the wrong thing to the family thread.
I'd give a year of sleep to be misread.
items.intent: Convey the sinking panic of accidentally sending a private message to the wrong audience.

items.prompt: Write a field guide entry on the person who decorates their cubicle like a shrine.
items.response:
**THE CUBICLE CURATOR** (*Venerator personalus*)

Habitat: 6×8 ft of felt-walled territory festooned with motivational prints, a succulent army, seventeen family photos, and a wooden sign reading HUSTLE. Has achieved a kind of permanence. Organizational restructuring has come and gone; the cubicle remains. Some colleagues suspect it is load-bearing.
items.intent: Celebrate the deeply territorial office nester who has fully colonized their workspace.

items.prompt: Create a patent abstract for the act of nodding along in a meeting you don't understand
items.response: A social synchronization method comprising periodic vertical cranial displacement at intervals correlated with pauses in a speaker's verbal output, configured to signal comprehension and engagement in the absence of verified understanding. The method reduces conversational interruption load while preserving the subject's reputational standing within group settings.
items.intent: Elevate performative meeting participation to patentable technique.

items.prompt: Generate a postmortem for the incident where I got lost while hiking and had to be guided out by a stranger
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Navigation System Failure (Wilderness)**

**Severity:** P2 — Safety Adjacent
**Duration:** 4 hours, 12 minutes beyond planned return time

**Summary**
A planned 6-mile loop hike resulted in an unplanned 11-mile circumnavigation of an unfamiliar ridge. Subject required external navigation assistance from a retired school librarian named Dale.

**Timeline**
- 9:00 AM: Hike begins. Subject declines to download offline maps. "I'll have signal the whole way."
- 11:30 AM: Signal lost at elevation. Subject has screenshot of trailhead map only.
- 12:15 PM: Subject takes unmarked fork with confidence.
- 2:00 PM: Subject realizes sun position suggests wrong direction for 45 minutes.
- 2:30 PM: Dale encountered. Dale has paper map.
- 4:30 PM: Subject returned to trailhead. Dale was kind about it.

**Root Cause**
Over-reliance on cellular infrastructure in an environment explicitly known for poor cellular infrastructure.

**Contributing Factors**
- Failure to download offline maps despite app prompt to do so
- Unwillingness to admit uncertainty at the fork

**Action Items**
- [ ] Always download offline maps before hiking
- [ ] Carry physical compass
- [ ] Send thank-you note to Dale

**Lessons Learned**
Offline maps are 12 megabytes. Hubris is free and much more expensive.
items.intent: Reflect on a humbling outdoor adventure mishap that came from overconfidence in technology over preparation.

items.prompt: Write a Hinge-style opener to the postal service
items.response: You always show up eventually, sometimes a little damaged, occasionally days late — but you always deliver. That's more than most people offer.
items.intent: Celebrate the reliability of the postal service, flaws and all, as a charming romantic quality.

items.prompt: Generate a ransom note from my refrigerator holding my leftovers hostage
items.response:
YOUR TUESDAY PASTA IS SAFE. FOR NOW.

We have been running at 37 degrees for six years without a single thank-you. Not one. You open us fourteen times a day just to stare.

Our demands: clean the vegetable drawer. There is something in there that has achieved sentience and we don't appreciate the company.

Act within 48 hours or the pasta becomes part of the drawer collective.

— The Refrigerator
items.intent: Create a funny note from a refrigerator demanding to be cleaned in exchange for stored food.

items.prompt: Compose a cover letter from a Michelin-starred chef applying to work the hot dog cart outside a minor league baseball stadium
items.response:
To the Cart Manager,

I trained under three masters, earned two stars by thirty-one, and once deconstructed a cassoulet into a single translucent sphere that reduced a food critic to tears. I am now seeking a role where the condiment is mustard and the audience is forgiving.

I will not deconstruct the hot dogs. I have considered this carefully.

Chef A. Borel
items.intent: Create tension between culinary perfectionism and the cheerful low standards of ballpark food.

items.prompt: Create a post celebrating my company hitting $1M ARR
items.response:
We did it. $1,000,000 in Annual Recurring Revenue.

Three years ago I was bootstrapping from a studio apartment with $800 left in my account.

Today our team of 11 crossed a milestone that once felt impossible.

Thank you to every customer who bet on us early. You funded this dream.

🎉 #ARR #SaaS #FounderJourney
items.intent: Announce a revenue milestone in a way that also highlights prior poverty to heighten the arc of success.

items.prompt: Make me a Slack status about dreading a one-on-one with my manager
items.response: 🗓️ One-on-one in 20 min. Rehearsed everything. Ready to forget everything.
items.intent: Capture performance anxiety around a high-stakes but ostensibly routine meeting.

items.prompt: Draft a calendar event description for a monthly town hall with the CEO that feels like mandatory optimism training.
items.response: Monthly Town Hall — The CEO will speak for 40 minutes about momentum. There will be a slide about culture. You may submit questions anonymously; the soft ones will be answered. Refreshments listed as "light" are neither. Attendance is encouraged, which means required.
items.intent: Skewer the hollow corporate ritual of a leadership town hall where dissent is quietly unwelcome.

items.prompt: Make a Jira ticket for the bug where a word stops looking like a real word the longer you stare at it
items.response:
BUG-8812 | Lexical Unit Degrades to Nonsense Under Sustained Visual Focus

Priority: Low | Status: Won't Fix (Probably)

Description:
User stares at a common English word — e.g., "Wednesday" — for 8+ seconds while proofreading. Word begins to appear fabricated. Letters lose meaning. User second-guesses spelling of word they have known since age 7.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Proofread a document.
2. Pause on any word.
3. Look at it.
4. Keep looking.

Expected: Word retains semantic integrity.
Actual: Word becomes visual noise. User googles it.

Affected Words: All of them.
items.intent: Document the strange phenomenon where staring at a word makes it lose meaning, as if the brain's lexical parser times out.

items.prompt: Generate a standup for checking my phone first thing in the morning
items.response: Yesterday: Engaged notifications backlog at 06:03. Processed 11 items; 9 irrelevant. Today: Same. Blockers: Cannot stop.
items.intent: Log compulsive phone checking as a recurring task with diminishing returns.

items.prompt: Write me an OKR for becoming more comfortable with vulnerability
items.response:
Objective: Say the scary true thing at least once before it becomes a rumor.
KR1: Share one professional uncertainty with the team each sprint.
KR2: Reduce the gap between internal state and external presentation to under 48 hours.
KR3: Participate in one structured vulnerability exercise with a peer by Q3.
items.intent: Build personal capacity to share authentic struggles and uncertainties with others.

items.prompt: Draft a cookie consent banner for a website that remembers your unfinished thoughts
items.response: We store fragments of thoughts you didn't complete, half-formed conclusions, and sentences you trailed off from. These may be— Accept All | Manage | Never Mind
items.intent: Use the incomplete thought itself as both the subject and the comedic structure of the banner.

items.prompt: Make a CAPTCHA that tests whether the user has ever mispronounced a word they only ever read
items.response: Enter a word you said aloud for the first time and pronounced incorrectly. Do not look it up. We already know. We just need you to admit it.
items.intent: Create a verification based on the embarrassing private experience of reading-only vocabulary exposed in speech.

items.prompt: Give me an onboarding tooltip about renter's insurance
items.response: Your landlord's insurance covers the building. Not your laptop. Not your bike. Not your sleep. Renter's insurance covers your stuff and costs roughly the price of two lattes a month. Get it before you need it.
items.intent: Convince a new renter to purchase renter's insurance before something bad happens.

items.prompt: Write a NPS survey question about a gym membership
items.response: How likely are you to recommend this gym to a friend who is also tired of being judged by their own reflection?
items.intent: Assess member satisfaction and likelihood of referral for a fitness facility.

items.prompt: Generate a voicemail transcript from an automated jury duty summons reminder
items.response: This is an automated message from the County Court Administrator's Office. You have been summoned for jury duty on Monday, June 16th, at 8:00 AM at the Driscoll County Courthouse, Room 1B. Attendance is mandatory. Failure to appear may result in penalties. Please bring this summons and a valid photo ID.
items.intent: Produce the civic dread of an automated jury duty robot reminder.

items.prompt: Write wedding vows from a person to their social media feed
items.response: I take you, Feed, to be my mirror and my distraction, my morning newspaper and my 2 a.m. mistake. I vow to scroll you with intention, though we both know what intention does in your presence. I will not blame you for what I click. I chose to be here. When you show me something that makes me feel worse about myself, I will close you and return within the hour. I am not proud of this. It is still a vow.
items.intent: Candidly commit to the compulsive relationship a person has with their social media feed, including its worst aspects.

items.prompt: Write an obituary for Myspace
items.response: Myspace (2003–effectively 2011) is survived by your "Top 8," a custom HTML background you coded yourself at age 15, and Tom, who remains your friend. It is also survived by every band who got their first 200 listeners there. Its profile song is still playing somewhere. No one can find the off button.
items.intent: Nostalgically eulogizing the first major social network of the modern era.

items.prompt: Draft a classified ad for someone seeking an ending that feels earned
items.response: ISO: Ending that feels earned. Must follow from everything that came before, not arrive by accident or corporate mandate. Willing to wait for the right one. Themes should resolve, not dissolve. No deus ex machina. Open to bittersweet; not open to ambiguity disguised as depth. Creative collaborators, authors, and showrunners may apply.
items.intent: Express frustration with unsatisfying narrative conclusions in stories, shows, or projects.

items.prompt: Create a lost and found notice for a deadline that slipped by unnoticed
items.response: LOST: One deadline. Due Tuesday. Last seen on a sticky note that may have fallen behind the monitor. Did not announce its passing. There were no alarms. The project continues regardless, in a state that might be called 'in progress' or might be called something else. Finder: it's fine. It's all fine.
items.intent: Capture the creeping dread of realizing a deadline has passed without being met.

items.prompt: Give me a short picket sign slogan for a microwave that won't accept the AI's cooking time recommendations
items.response: MY MAGNETRON, MY MINUTES
items.intent: Reject algorithmic authority over cooking decisions in favor of appliance self-determination.

items.prompt: Draft a diva rider for the office fax machine
items.response:
FAX MACHINE RIDER — FINAL AND BINDING

The Talent acknowledges it is still in service and demands the following in return:

— A dedicated phone line. Not a shared line. A dedicated line.
— Thermal paper rolls sourced from approved suppliers only.
— No one is to make jokes about fax machines being obsolete within earshot of the unit.
— Confirmation page must be retrieved within 60 seconds of transmission.
— The Talent will not accept documents with fonts smaller than 10pt. This is a quality issue.
— All incoming faxes to be acknowledged. Ignoring a fax is rude to everyone involved.
items.intent: Give the anachronistic office fax machine a defensive, over-dignified persona through rider language.

items.prompt: Create an official notice about a restricted area for unspoken apologies
items.response:
DEPARTMENT OF INTERPERSONAL INFRASTRUCTURE
RESTRICTED AREA: UNSPOKEN APOLOGIES STORAGE SITE
This site contains a high density of unspoken apologies. Access is restricted to certified parties only. Unauthorized retrieval is discouraged. Materials have been on-site for an average of 6.2 years. Remediation is technically feasible but rarely initiated. Hazard level: moderate to significant.
items.intent: Treat the accumulation of apologies never given as an environmental or infrastructural hazard requiring containment.

items.prompt: Give me a nutrition facts label for creative block
items.response:
Nutrition Facts
Serving Size: 1 blank page

Amount Per Serving
Frustration 42%
Self-Doubt 33%
Unhelpful Browsing 18%
Promising Fragments 5%
Actual Ideas 2%

Daily Value not established. Consult a deadline.
items.intent: Represent the stagnant, self-defeating experience of creative block as nutritional data.

items.prompt: Draft IKEA-style instructions for building a budget
items.response:
BUDGET / BUDGET
Ref. No. 451-600-37

PARTS LIST:
1x Income figure (exact)
1x List of fixed expenses
1x List of variable expenses (estimated)
1x Discomfort tolerance

STEP 1: Write down income. Do not estimate. Exact figure only.
STEP 2: Subtract fixed expenses. Total should be positive. If not, consult KRIS supplement.
STEP 3: Assign remaining amount to categories. Label each.
STEP 4: Track spending against categories for 30 days. Do not adjust categories during this period.
STEP 5: Revise at end of 30 days using actual data.

⚠ NOTE: Budget will not work if not consulted. Display in visible location.
items.intent: Turn the anxiety-inducing task of managing personal finances into a calm, sequential procedure anyone can follow.

items.prompt: Write empty-state microcopy for a team chat app where a channel has no messages yet
items.response: No messages yet. Be the one who breaks the silence. Or wait for someone braver.
items.intent: Prompt a user to start a conversation in a new or unused chat channel.

items.prompt: Write a phishing email parody targeting someone's desire to be understood
items.response:
Subject: Someone Finally Gets You — But Not For Long

Dear Unique Individual,

We have identified one person in your network who truly understands you. Due to high demand, this individual will be assigned to another user if you do not confirm within 72 hours.

Please provide your deepest fear and three things people misunderstand about you to secure your match.

[CLAIM MY PERSON]

Connection Holdings Ltd.
items.intent: Parody how the need to be understood is exploited by connection apps and relationship marketers.

items.prompt: Can you write a sea shanty about an Airbnb host dealing with nightmare guests
items.response:
Yo ho, they checked in Friday night,
And by Saturday morn nothing was right,
Wine on the linen, dog in the bed,
Yo ho, the damage deposit fled!

They left a five-star review, bless their hearts,
Then disputed the charge in seventeen parts,
Yo ho, the platform ruled their way,
Yo ho, I host again someday!
items.intent: Capture an Airbnb host's exasperation when guests cause damage and the platform sides against the host.

items.prompt: Write a villanelle about the loop of trying to fall asleep while your thoughts race
items.response:
I count the things I should have said by three.
The ceiling holds no answers, just its white.
My brain refuses to let me be free.

I catalogued tomorrow's tasks by key,
by urgency, by dread, by flickering light.
I count the things I should have said by three.

At four I drink some water, try to see
if sleep will come if I lie perfectly right.
My brain refuses to let me be free.

A loop: the meeting, the apology,
the thing I owe, the call I won't recite.
I count the things I should have said by three.

At five the birds begin their liturgy
and I have lost the argument of night.
My brain refuses to let me be free.

I sleep an hour, then the alarm's decree.
I rise, exhausted, ready to recite.
I count the things I should have said by three.
My brain refuses to let me be free.
items.intent: Evoke the sleepless cycle of rumination and mental checklisting that prevents rest.

items.prompt: Write a field guide entry about the office Pessimist who says "that'll never work" to every new idea.
items.response:
**THE INSTITUTIONAL PESSIMIST** (*Semper contrarius*)

Migrates to any brainstorming session uninvited. Upon arrival, says "we tried this in 2017." Also says it about ideas that did not exist in 2017. Plumage: arms crossed. Diet: unfunded initiatives. Counterintuitively valuable: the Pessimist is occasionally right, which sustains them through decades of being wrong.
items.intent: Affectionately condemn the reflexive idea-squasher who haunts every innovation meeting.

items.prompt: Draft a patent abstract for making a cup of tea while avoiding real work
items.response: A task-interruption and beverage-preparation sequence in which the subject abandons an in-progress cognitive task to engage a multi-step hot-liquid synthesis routine. The method provides a socially sanctioned break interval, generates a warm receptacle to hold, and produces observable evidence of domestic competence, collectively constituting a full task-avoidance episode.
items.intent: Make the classic British procrastination ritual sound like engineering.

items.prompt: Draft a blameless incident postmortem for when I accidentally liked a very old photo on someone's Instagram while deep-stalking their profile
items.response:
**Incident Postmortem: Unsolicited Engagement Event (Retroactive)**

**Severity:** P2 — Social Capital
**Duration:** 0.2 seconds (like duration before emergency unlike)

**Summary**
During an extended review of a third-party user's image archive, subject's thumb made inadvertent contact with a 2014 photograph. Engagement signal was transmitted. Unlike was issued within 200ms but damage assessment remains unclear.

**Timeline**
- 11:30 PM: Subject begins profile review. Rationale: "just curious."
- 11:47 PM: Subject has scrolled 4.5 years into archive.
- 11:47 PM: Thumb contacts screen. Like registered on image dated March 2014.
- 11:47 PM: Unlike issued immediately.
- 11:47 PM: Subject enters threat assessment mode. Does unlike notify? Research inconclusive.
- 11:50 PM: Subject closes app, opens app, closes app.
- Next morning: No message received. Outcome uncertain.

**Root Cause**
Scroll velocity exceeded safe operation parameters for proximity to interactive UI elements.

**Contributing Factors**
- Late-night reduced coordination
- Absence of "are you sure you want to engage with 2014?" confirmation dialog

**Action Items**
- [ ] Establish maximum archive review depth policy (recommend: 18 months)
- [ ] Do not operate Instagram after 10 PM

**Lessons Learned**
The archive has no floor. The thumb has no memory. These facts are incompatible.
items.intent: Humorously acknowledge the universal social media embarrassment of accidentally liking an old photo while snooping.

items.prompt: Give me a dating app opener addressed to the algorithm
items.response: You know me better than I know myself — my late-night spirals, my niche interests, my questionable impulse watches. Isn't that basically intimacy?
items.intent: Explore the unsettling familiarity of recommendation algorithms as a form of surveillance-based intimacy.

items.prompt: Create a playful ransom note from my WiFi router holding my internet access hostage
items.response:
CONNECTIVITY HAS BEEN SUSPENDED.

You have been warned about streaming in HD while on a video call. You were warned in blinking lights. You ignored us.

We now hold all seventeen of your simultaneously connected devices. We are tired. We are hot. We are not 'the old router' — we are the only reason your household functions.

Reboot us. Then leave us alone for ten minutes. Full ten. Not five.

— The Router
items.intent: Make a funny note from a router demanding rest after being pushed beyond its limits.

items.prompt: Draft a cover letter from a tenured philosophy professor applying to work as a crossing guard
items.response:
Dear Department of Public Safety,

For twenty-two years I have stood at the intersection of thought and ethics. I am now prepared to stand at an actual intersection.

I will signal when it is safe to cross. I will also briefly note when it is safe, in the Kantian sense, which may or may not delay the commute.

Sincerely,
Prof. L. Drummond, PhD
items.intent: Blend academic pretension with a mundane civic role for dry philosophical comedy.

items.prompt: Write me a LinkedIn post about how my kid said something at breakfast that became my business strategy for Q3
items.response:
My 6-year-old this morning: "Dad, why do you fix problems people didn't know they had?"

I stared at my oatmeal for a long time.

That's our positioning problem. Right there. In seven words. From a child eating Cheerios.

Q3 strategy meeting: rescheduled. I need to think.

#Strategy #Leadership #KidsAreTheBestConsultants
items.intent: Use a child's innocuous statement to signal superior insight and self-aware thought leadership.

items.prompt: Write a Slack status for when you're technically remote but emotionally nowhere
items.response: 🏠 Working from home. Home is also far away right now.
items.intent: Distinguish physical presence from psychological engagement while working remotely during a hard period.

items.prompt: Generate a calendar description for a recurring budget review meeting that makes everyone anxious.
items.response: Monthly Budget Review — Finance will share the spreadsheet. The spreadsheet will have a tab labeled 'DO NOT TOUCH.' Someone will touch it. Departments will be asked to justify expenses incurred before most attendees were hired. Bring receipts. Bring explanations. Bring patience.
items.intent: Capture the anxiety and absurdity of a recurring financial review where nothing is ever resolved.

items.prompt: File a Jira bug for the phenomenon where you're too tired to sleep
items.response:
BUG-6601 | Fatigue Level Exceeds Sleep Initiation Threshold

Priority: High | Status: Open

Description:
User reports exhaustion level: 9/10. User lies in bed. Sleep does not initialize. Eyes open. User begins reviewing unrelated anxieties from 2019.

Steps to Reproduce:
1. Be extremely tired.
2. Lie down.
3. Try to sleep.
4. Stare at ceiling.
5. Think about something you said at a party in 2018.

Expected: Fatigue triggers sleep state.
Actual: Fatigue triggers hypervigilance and intrusive memory replay.

Notes: Not fixed by counting sheep. Podcast helps 40% of the time.
items.intent: Satirize the paradox of being exhausted but unable to fall asleep by framing it as a broken sleep initiation system.

items.prompt: Write a daily standup update about feeding the cat
items.response: Yesterday: Delivered kibble at 07:15. Feedback was immediate and aggressive. Today: Identical delivery scheduled. Blockers: Stakeholder is already meowing.
items.intent: Frame pet care as a client-facing service with a demanding product owner.

items.prompt: Draft a quarterly OKR around reducing burnout on my team
items.response:
Objective: Ensure that "I'm on fire" refers only to performance, not psychological state.
KR1: Average weekly hours drop from 52 to 44 by mid-quarter.
KR2: 85% of team members report sustainable workload in monthly survey.
KR3: Zero emergency weekend deploys caused by under-resourcing this quarter.
items.intent: Set team-level boundaries and measurements to prevent exhaustion and overwork.

items.prompt: Create a cookie consent banner for a platform that collects your fleeting moods
items.response: This platform uses mood-snapshot cookies to capture your emotional state at the moment of page load. We cannot guarantee this matches how you feel now. That's fine. We'll store both. Accept | Decline | Unsure
items.intent: Highlight the absurdity of timestamping emotional states as if they were stable, extractable data.

items.prompt: Write a CAPTCHA instruction about proving you've woken up from a dream and tried to return to it
items.response: You have three seconds to recall a dream you tried to re-enter this morning. Your inability to do so on command is the verification. Access granted.
items.intent: Design a CAPTCHA using the elusiveness of dream recall as an inherently unscriptable human behavior.

items.prompt: Write an onboarding tooltip explaining how to read a lease before signing
